overview: From 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-01, elizaos/eliza had 34 new PRs (12 merged), 3 new issues, and 12 active contributors.

topIssues.title: ACP -32602 'Invalid params' failures are undiagnosable (JSON-RPC error.data discarded)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body: When a spawned ACP sub-agent returns JSON-RPC -32602 'Invalid params', the failing-field detail lives in the error.data payload (e.g. a ZodError.format()). The native ACP transport's jsonRpcError() keeps only error.message and drops error.data, so failures surface as a bare 'Invalid params' with no way to identify the offending field. error.data should be preserved and surfaced.

topIssues.title: non-Anthropic models (gpt-oss/Cerebras) have no inline live-info capability
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body: On a non-Anthropic provider (e.g. gpt-oss/Cerebras), the keyless Anthropic web-search monkey-patch is skipped (isAnthropicRuntime() is false) and the cloud webSearch action requires a GOOGLE/GEMINI key. With neither, the main agent has no inline action to answer 'what is the current price of X / weather' and degrades to a 'no live access' decline, forcing delegation to a coding sub-agent that writes scripts instead of fetching. The agent needs a keyless, SSRF-guarded inline fetch capability so live-info works on any provider in local-only mode.

topIssues.title: develop fails to build after the requireConfirmation two-phase rollout
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body: develop does not pass 'turbo run build': (1) plugin-github issue-op/pr-op confirmation handlers return a success result with text + a confirmation/cancelled data payload, but GitHubActionResult's success variant lacks 'text' and the *OpResult unions lack those members (TS2353); (2) core's requireConfirmation is exported only from index.node.ts, not index.browser.ts, though it is browser-safe and plugin-wallet imports it, breaking example-trader-ts's browser bundle with MISSING_EXPORT.

topPRs.title: Claude/agentic views surface
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
# Relates to # Risks # Background ## What does this PR do? ## What kind of change is this? # Documentation changes needed? # Testing ## Where should a reviewer start? ## Detailed testing steps

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces an **agent-surface framework** that makes every plugin view agent-controllable (registry, overlay, `useAgentElement` hook, and capability dispatch), plus a **view-action affinity layer** that upweights view-scoped runtime actions in the planner's prompt while the user is looking at that view. It also ships **sleep/wake lifecycle endpoints** for deep cold-storage of agent compute, **incremental diff backups** for agent sandboxes, and a **scheduled-backup cron** to keep agents continuously snapshotted. - Agent-surface: `ViewAgentRegistry`, `AgentSurfaceProvider`, `useAgentElement`, `AgentElementOverlay`, and capability handlers wired into both `DynamicViewLoader` and the new `ShellViewAgentSurface` wrapper. - Incremental backups: `agent-backup-diff.ts` computes field-level deltas; migration 0136 adds `backup_kind`/`parent_backup_id`/`content_hash` safely. - Sleep/wake: `executeSleep` backs up then frees compute; `executeWake` re-provisions and restores.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge; the most consequential new paths are well-tested and the DB change is backwards-compatible. The three main feature areas all land with test coverage and the migration is safe. The pre-shutdown backup inconsistency leaves content_hash null on the most important backup rows, but is functionally safe since the column is nullable. packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts — the executeSleep pre-shutdown backup path

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/agent/src/runtime/view-action-affinity.ts | New module mapping active plugin views to preferred runtime actions. | | packages/ui/src/agent-surface/registry.ts | New ViewAgentRegistry; AgentSurfaceProvider properly calls removeViewRegistry on unmount. | | packages/cloud-api/v1/eliza/agents/[agentId]/sleep/route.ts | New sleep endpoint; returns 202 for both new and already-in-progress jobs. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts | Adds executeSleep/executeWake. Pre-shutdown backup bypasses buildBackupInput, omitting content_hash. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/agent-backup-diff.ts | New incremental backup diffing/patching library; logic is correct and well-tested. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/db/migrations/0136_agent_backup_incremental.sql | Adds backup_kind/parent_backup_id/content_hash columns safely with IF NOT EXISTS and DEFAULT 'full'. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Shell as Shell participant Registry as ViewAgentRegistry participant Affinity as view-action-affinity participant Optimizer as prompt-optimization participant Agent as Planner Shell->>Registry: getOrCreateViewRegistry() Shell->>Affinity: "setActiveViewContext({viewId})" Agent->>Shell: "capability=list-elements" Shell->>Registry: snapshot() Registry-->>Agent: elements[] Optimizer->>Affinity: getActiveViewContext() Affinity-->>Optimizer: activeView Optimizer->>Agent: prompt with Active View block ``` Reviews (3): Last reviewed commit: ["test(agent): cover character + automatio..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/0424b6a57cf997545a078bd930228b2795715512) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34774957)

Block MCP stdio env injection, sanitize shell child env, verify WhatsApp/BlueBubbles webhooks with rawBody, and add defense-in-depth for browser workspace, computeruse file ops, incoming public messages, and vendored sweagent paths.

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator): live Claude-Code-style coding workbench (streaming, diffs, stop, SSE)
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## What Brings the `/orchestrator` coding workbench to live, Claude-Code/Codex-style parity, end-to-end on the **opencode + Cerebras (gpt-oss-120b)** backend. Each commit is gate-green (tsgo + biome + vitest) and proven in a real headless-Chrome session. ## Commits 1. **auto-spawn opencode+cerebras on first message** — messaging a task with no live agent now spawns one (was the unimplemented `elizaos` default → `spawn ENOENT`); default framework → vendored opencode; per-task workdir. 2. **Claude-Code-style streaming workbench view** — coalesces streamed stdout chunks into one markdown turn; renders `tool_running` events as structured tool-call cards; typed status notices; finished-session tools resolve to ✓; fixes cross-task transcript contamination. 3. **key view-manager cards by viewType+id** — removes 9 duplicate-React-key console errors. 4. **real interleaved diff viewer** — LCS line-diff with old/new gutters + +/-/context coloring (replaces the flat all-removals-then-all-additions); unit-tested. 5. **Stop / Esc interrupt** — a prominent "Agent working… / Stop (Esc)" bar; global Esc cancels the run (proven: stopped a run in ~0.5s, work halted 5/12 files). 6. **test fixes** — update two unit tests to the auto-spawn + opencode-default behavior. 7. **live SSE transport** — per-task change bus + `GET /tasks/:taskId/stream` SSE endpoint + an EventSource client; the room updates live (sub-second) instead of polling, polling kept only as reconnect fallback. ## Proof (headless Chrome, opencode+cerebras) - Tool-call cards + coalesced markdown, **0 legacy fragments**, **0 duplicate-key console errors**. - Real interleaved diff on a live edit (`- sugar is sweet` / `+ and so are you`). - Esc interrupted a run mid-work (5/12 files, ~0.5s). - SSE: `ready` + 23 `change` pings over a run; the workbench's EventSource subscribes and the conversation grows sub-second. - `tsgo` + `biome` + `vitest` (503 plugin tests) green. ## Backend Coding backend stays **opencode + Cerebras** (vendored, gpt-oss-120b). No Claude/Codex backend. ## Follow-ups (next PRs) Interactive permission round-trip, live plan/todo checklist, multi-backend normalization (claude/codex), and the P1 set (changed-files nav, slash menu, @-file refs, cost meter, ANSI terminal, keyboard overlay, error/retry cards). 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Greptile Summary

This PR wires up a Claude-Code/Codex-style live coding workbench on top of the orchestrator plugin: SSE push transport replaces polling, streamed agent chunks are coalesced into coherent turns, tool calls render as structured diff/shell cards, a stop/Esc interrupt bar halts running agents, and the LCS-based `lineDiff` produces real interleaved diffs instead of flat remove-then-add blocks. - **SSE transport** (`orchestrator-routes.ts`, `client-agent.ts`): per-task pub/sub bus with `subscribeTaskChanges`; heartbeat keeps proxies alive; debounced client-side refetch on each `change` ping; polling retained as reconnect fallback. - **Streaming workbench** (`orchestrator-stream.tsx`): `buildConversation` merges same-lane agent chunks, collapses multi-event tool calls by ID, and resolves perpetual spinners for finished sessions; `MarkdownText` renders inline code/bold/links and fenced code blocks. - **Auto-spawn** (`orchestrator-task-service.ts`): messaging a task with no live agent now spawns an opencode+Cerebras session automatically; the vendored opencode backend replaces the unimplemented elizaos default.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge with one fix: the EventSource client needs an onerror handler to prevent infinite reconnect loops when the task is deleted or returns a non-2xx response. The SSE client in `streamOrchestratorTask` has no `onerror` handler. When the server returns a 404 (task not found or deleted) or any other error, the browser's EventSource automatically retries every ~3 seconds indefinitely, hammering the endpoint until the component unmounts. The polling fallback is already in place, so simply closing the EventSource on error loses no functionality. Everything else — the conversation builder, diff renderer, stop/Esc bar, cross-task contamination fix, and auto-spawn path — looks correct and well-guarded. packages/ui/src/api/client-agent.ts — the `streamOrchestratorTask` function needs an `onerror` handler that calls `source.close()`.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/ui/src/api/client-agent.ts | Adds `streamOrchestratorTask` SSE subscription helper; missing `onerror` handler causes infinite reconnect loop on permanent server errors (404/403/5xx). | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/api/orchestrator-routes.ts | Adds GET /tasks/:taskId/stream SSE endpoint with heartbeat, per-task pub/sub, and correct cleanup on req close/error; double-cleanup is guarded correctly. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/orchestrator-task-service.ts | Adds in-memory per-task change bus, auto-spawn on first message, and opencode default; `emitChange` defensively wraps subscribers; no cleanup of listeners on service stop (minor leak). | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/workdir-validation.ts | Adds `ensureTaskWorkdir` that creates `~/.eliza/workspaces/` for auto-spawning; path is constructed with `path.resolve` before mkdir, staying within the workspace base. | | plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/orchestrator-stream.tsx | New file: builds the streaming conversation view — coalesces same-lane agent chunks, merges tool events by ID, renders tool-call cards, inline markdown, and structured notices. Logic is sound; FENCE regex lastIndex is reset correctly before each use. | | plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/orchestrator-diff.tsx | New file: LCS-based interleaved line diff with old/new gutter numbers; falls back to flat remove-then-add above 800 combined lines; unit-tested; keys are unique within each invocation. | | plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/OrchestratorWorkbench.tsx | Major workbench refactor: switches to `buildConversation`, adds SSE subscription, debounced refetch, adaptive poll interval, stop/Esc bar, and cross-task contamination fix; stickToBottom scroll guard is correct. | | packages/ui/src/components/pages/ViewManagerPage.tsx | Fixes duplicate React key warnings by compositing `viewType` + `id` in both ViewSection and TopViewsSection. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/__tests__/unit/orchestrator-task-service.test.ts | Updates two tests to match auto-spawn behavior and opencode default provider; assertions are consistent with the new runtime behavior. | | plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/__tests__/unit/orchestrator-diff.test.ts | New unit tests for `lineDiff` covering unchanged, single-line change, insertion, deletion, and mixed-edit cases; all edge cases produce correct old/new line numbers. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/setup-routes.ts | Registers the new SSE route path so it receives framework-level auth middleware identical to other orchestrator endpoints. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Browser as Browser (OrchestratorWorkbench) participant API as orchestrator-routes (SSE) participant Service as OrchestratorTaskService participant Agent as opencode Agent Browser->>API: GET /tasks/:taskId/stream (EventSource) API->>Service: subscribeTaskChanges(taskId, listener) API-->>Browser: "data: {type:"ready"}" loop Agent working Agent->>Service: session event / tool_running / usage Service->>Service: emitChange(taskId) Service->>API: listener() fires API-->>Browser: "data: {type:"change"}" Browser->>Browser: scheduleRefetch (debounce 150ms) Browser->>API: GET /tasks/:taskId/messages + events API-->>Browser: updated conversation tail Browser->>Browser: buildConversation → render end Browser->>API: Stop (Esc / button) API->>Service: stopOrchestratorAgent(taskId, sessionId) Service->>Agent: terminate Browser->>Browser: component unmount / task switch Browser->>API: EventSource.close() API->>API: req close → cleanup (clearInterval + unsubscribe + res.end) ``` Reviews (6): Last reviewed commit: ["feat(orchestrator): premium UI pass — la..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/63f569ecf8e9e8b7ba0799c6d27fae203a1405e0) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34800292)

topPRs.title: fix(orchestrator): ground sub-agent completion in the real change set + stop respawn cascade
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Problem 1. A false `state_lost` probe plus unbounded respawn could cascade duplicate sub-agents. 2. Asked "what did you change / show the diff", the parent confabulated edits because completion narration wasn't grounded in the actual change set. ## Change - Bound respawn and fix the `state_lost` false-positive (stops the cascade). - Capture the real change set from git (`name-status` vs the spawn baseline) unioned with the agent's edit/write tool-call paths (covers gitignored deploy targets), excluding files already dirty at spawn. - Surface it via a provider gated by a new general `Provider.alwaysInResponseState` flag (reaches the simple-reply path without core naming the plugin), with a staleness guard that won't surface an older change set once a newer task has run. - Dispatch the coding sub-agent when the planner names a simile of the TASKS action. ## Testing orchestrator suite green (393); `workspace-diff`/`coding-session-changes`/`sub-agent-router`/`acp-service` unit tests green; core message-routing/stage1/trajectory green.

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes two interconnected orchestrator bugs: a false-positive `state_lost` health-check probe (probing `.stream.ndjson` which was never written for native sessions, flipping every idle `ready` session to `errored` and triggering unbounded `session_state_lost` respawn cascades), and completion narrations that confabulated edits because the parent read the sub-agent's raw tool transcript instead of the real change set. - **Health-check gate**: `runHealthCheck` now skips any session not in `ACP_MIDFLIGHT_SESSION_STATUSES` (`running`/`busy`/`tool_running`), fixing the false-positive cascade trigger; the probe target is also corrected from `.stream.ndjson` to the actual `.json` artifact. - **Respawn backstop**: `SubAgentRouter` now tracks `session_state_lost` events per stable origin lineage (`taskRoomId + agentType`) and caps cross-session respawn at `DEFAULT_STATE_LOST_RESPAWN_CAP = 3`, resetting on a genuine `task_complete`. - **Ground-truth change set**: At `task_complete`, `workspace-diff.ts` captures git `--name-status` output plus agent tool-call paths, persisted to session metadata and surfaced via a new `CODING_SESSION_CHANGES` provider flagged with `alwaysInResponseState: true`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

The core bug fixes are correct and well-tested; two open issues from prior review threads remain unaddressed in the new change-set capture and staleness-guard code paths. The health-check fix and the respawn cap are clear correctional improvements. The open issues from prior review threads represent cases where the new functionality silently degrades: the staleness guard never fires when sessions are loaded from a database (createdAt is an ISO string, not a Date), and the dirtyAtSpawn filter can misidentify agent-written files that were also dirty at spawn time for absolute-path tool calls. plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/providers/coding-session-changes.ts (staleness guard) and plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/workspace-diff.ts (dirtyAtSpawn exclusion path-format mismatch)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/providers/coding-session-changes.ts | New provider surfacing real git change set for show-me-the-diff queries; staleness guard silently disabled for DB-backed runtimes (createdAt instanceof Date fails on ISO strings). | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/workspace-diff.ts | New module capturing the real git change set at task completion; dirtyAtSpawn exclusion filter compares raw toolPaths against git-relative baselineDirty entries, broken for absolute-path tool calls. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/acp-service.ts | Health-check now correctly skips non-mid-flight sessions; probes the correct .json artifact instead of .stream.ndjson. Fix is minimal and targeted. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/sub-agent-router.ts | Adds per-lineage state_lost respawn cap (default 3), cross-session task_complete deduplication, and real git change set capture at task_complete. Logic is sound. | | packages/core/src/services/message.ts | Adds alwaysOnResponseStateProviderNames helper and simile-aware exposedActionMatches for candidate action validation. Changes are correct and well-tested. | | packages/core/src/types/components.ts | Adds alwaysInResponseState optional flag to the Provider interface. Backward-compatible extension. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/index.ts | Registers codingSessionChangesProvider in orchestratorProviders gated by codeExecutionAllowed; clean integration. | Reviews (6): Last reviewed commit: ["test(core): cover simile-aware candidate..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/c0201397b9a92a7633f48b83f792a77f564ab4d8) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34557249)

topPRs.title: feat(cloud): fleet-upgrade reconciler — blue/green swap onto the deployed image
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
## Summary Wire up a real fleet rollout for cloud agents. When the registry-side digest of the configured agent tag moves, the daemon now enqueues a blue/green \`agent_upgrade\` job per running agent still on the old digest, capped at 3 in flight, and executes them as zero-downtime cross-node swaps. Today's reality: a new image only reaches the fleet when an agent crashes or someone restarts it manually, so the cluster runs a mix of versions until everyone naturally cycles. The morning of 2026-05-29 had agents on \`develop\` alongside agents on \`eliza-tla:patched\` for that reason. ## How it works - **Probe**: the reconciler calls \`resolveImageDigest(containersEnv.defaultAgentImage())\` against ghcr.io every 30s. Cached 60s. Null on bare image names, non-ghcr registries, or registry errors — those cycles are no-ops, the agent stays put. - **Pick a target node**: \`getAvailableNode({ excludeNodeId: currentNode })\`. Different node guarantees no container-name collision (names are deterministic \`agent-\${id}\` per docker daemon). Autoscaler-on-demand path fires automatically if no other node has free capacity. - **Provision blue + health check**: regular provisioner path with the new image. If it never goes healthy, we tear down blue and bail — the agent keeps running on old with old image. Auto-rollback. - **Atomic swap**: one UPDATE on \`agent_sandboxes\` (bridge_url, health_url, node_id, container_name, ports, docker_image, image_digest). New HTTP requests hit blue from this point on. - **Drain + remove old**: \`docker stop -t 30\` SIGTERM (30s drain for in-flight HTTP + websockets), \`docker rm -f\`. Allocated count decremented on the old node. - **Cleanup**: the autoscaler's existing idle-node drain handles nodes that empty out as agents rebalance. ## Schema New \`image_digest text\` column on \`agent_sandboxes\`, stamped at provision time. NULL on rows that predate this PR is treated as "differs from target" — first rollout sweeps the existing fleet onto the current digest, after which the registry-vs-stored compare is exact. Migration 0135, additive, no backfill required. ## Test plan - [x] 17 unit tests on the registry probe — parsing edge cases (port-in-registry, bare image names, empty tag, malformed), 404 / 5xx / network-error handling, only-ghcr policy, positive + negative caching with TTL expiry verification. - [x] Smoke test on the job-type registry — \`AGENT_UPGRADE\` is registered and unique. - [x] \`biome check\` clean on all changed files. - [x] \`typecheck\` clean for the changed files. - [ ] Manual end-to-end on staging: push a fresh \`:develop\`, watch the reconciler enqueue + execute 3 swaps per cycle, verify blue boots healthy, swap is atomic, old container gets a SIGTERM drain. ## Out of scope (follow-ups) - Per-agent opt-out flag (some agents may need to be pinned to a digest). - Idle-aware scheduling (skip swap when an agent has an active websocket). - Webhook from \`build-agent-image.yml\` to trigger an immediate rollout rather than waiting for the 30s reconciler tick.

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces a fleet-upgrade reconciler that detects when the ghcr.io digest of the configured agent tag has moved and performs rolling blue/green swaps across the fleet, capped at 3 concurrent upgrades. It adds a `registry-probe` module for cached digest resolution, a new `AGENT_UPGRADE` job type with full enqueue/execute machinery, `image_digest` stamping on `agent_sandboxes`, and cross-node placement via `excludeNodeId`. - **`registry-probe.ts`**: 60 s cached HEAD-based digest lookup against ghcr.io only; returns `null` for non-ghcr refs and all error paths; 17 unit tests cover parsing edge cases, HTTP failure modes, cache TTL, and negative caching. - **`executeUpgrade` in `eliza-sandbox.ts`**: provisions a blue container on a different node, health-checks it, atomically swaps the DB row, then best-effort SIGTERMs the old container; auto-rollback if blue doesn't come up healthy. - **`processFleetUpgradeCycle` in `provisioning-worker.ts`**: per-tick reconciler that probes the registry, counts in-flight jobs, queries candidates via `IS DISTINCT FROM`, and enqueues up to the remaining capacity slack.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge with one fix: concurrent stop/suspend/delete can race with an in-flight upgrade and leave an orphaned running container `hasActiveProvisionJobTx` only queries for `AGENT_PROVISION` jobs, so user-triggered stop, suspend, or delete operations are not blocked while a 60-90 s blue provisioning is in progress. If a stop lands first, the upgrade's unconditional swap UPDATE writes the blue container's coordinates into the stopped row, leaving a running container that receives no traffic and has no automatic cleanup path unless the idle-node autoscaler eventually drains that node. Everything else is solid. packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts — specifically `hasActiveProvisionJobTx` and the atomic swap UPDATE in `executeUpgrade`

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts | Adds `executeUpgrade` for blue/green agent swap; lifecycle lock and hasActiveProvisionJobTx guard are missing for AGENT_UPGRADE jobs, enabling orphaned blue containers on concurrent stop/delete | | packages/scripts/cloud/admin/daemons/provisioning-worker.ts | Adds `processFleetUpgradeCycle` reconciler — sound design, rate-limited, uses registry probe | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/containers/registry-probe.ts | New ghcr.io digest probe with 60 s cache, ghcr-only guard, URL-encoded paths — well-structured with comprehensive test coverage | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/docker-sandbox-provider.ts | Adds `stopOnSpecificNode` (best-effort drain) and `imageDigest` stamping at provision time | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/provisioning-jobs.ts | Adds `AgentUpgradeJobData`, `enqueueAgentUpgradeOnce`, and `executeAgentUpgrade` — consistent with existing job patterns | | packages/cloud-shared/src/db/repositories/agent-sandboxes.ts | Adds `listRunningWithDigestOtherThan` using IS DISTINCT FROM for null-safe comparison and pool exclusion | | packages/cloud-shared/src/db/migrations/0135_agent_sandboxes_image_digest.sql | Additive migration: nullable `image_digest text` column with IF NOT EXISTS guard — safe to deploy |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant R as Reconciler (30s tick) participant Reg as ghcr.io participant DB as agent_sandboxes (DB) participant Jobs as jobs table participant W as Worker participant OldNode as Old Node participant NewNode as New Node R->>Reg: resolveImageDigest(defaultAgentImage) Reg-->>R: targetDigest (sha256:...) R->>Jobs: countInFlightByType("agent_upgrade") Jobs-->>R: inFlight count R->>DB: listRunningWithDigestOtherThan(targetDigest, slack) DB-->>R: candidates[] R->>Jobs: enqueueAgentUpgradeOnce() per candidate W->>Jobs: pick up agent_upgrade job W->>DB: findByIdAndOrg (snapshot current state) W->>NewNode: "provider.create(excludeNodeId=oldNodeId)" NewNode-->>W: blueHandle + metadata W->>NewNode: checkHealth(blueHandle) alt health check fails W->>NewNode: provider.stop(blueHandle) rollback W-->>Jobs: mark failed (old container untouched) else healthy W->>DB: UPDATE agent_sandboxes SET node_id, container_name, bridge_url, image_digest Note over W,DB: Atomic swap - new requests hit blue W->>OldNode: stopOnSpecificNode(30s SIGTERM drain) OldNode-->>W: container removed W->>Jobs: mark completed end ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/docker-sandbox-provider.ts`, line 627-632 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/bba2d0185138bef187618b8309291075b77904cc/packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/docker-sandbox-provider.ts#L627-L632)) **Overcommit risk when old-node SSH is unreachable** `decrementAllocated` runs unconditionally after the stop/rm attempts, even if both fail (e.g., a network partition to the old node). If the SSH calls throw and the container stays alive, `allocated_count` on the old node is decremented past its real container count. Subsequent scheduling decisions will treat that node as having more free capacity than it actually has, and the next agent provisioned there can exceed the node's actual limits. The autoscaler's idle-drain will eventually reconcile, but the gap can span multiple reconciler cycles on a busy fleet.
Reviews (2): Last reviewed commit: ["feat(cloud): fleet-upgrade reconciler — ..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/26616f863db648680f7f2e39aa20043b57bd0b34) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34576839)

completedItems.title: test(elizaos): smoke global package install
completedItems.body:

Fixes/prevents regression for #8000

# Risks

Low. This only extends the existing packaged CLI smoke test to also install the packed tarball globally into an isolated temporary npm prefix before running the generated `elizaos`

completedItems.title: fix(core): keep the original stage-1 reply when regeneration returns empty
completedItems.body:
## Problem
When a stage-1 reply was regenerated and the regeneration returned an empty string, the original (valid) reply was dropped — so a correct terse answer (e.g. a bare number) could be lost.

topContributors.summary: 0xSolace: Drove significant infrastructure and cloud integration improvements for the elizaos/eliza project, most notably by implementing containerized agent provisioning in PR #8073 and wiring critical webhook and spend-tracking features in PRs #8094 and #8089. Their work involved extensive modifications across 428 files, balancing complex feature development with essential CI/CD bug fixes to ensure agent image stability.

NubsCarson: Drove significant stability and feature expansion across the elizaos/eliza repository, merging critical fixes for core structural logic (#8071) and CI release contracts (#8072) while managing a high volume of active development across 13 open PRs. Their work today was primarily focused on bugfixes (59%) and feature implementation (35%), spanning extensive codebase modifications to improve orchestrator reliability, agent provisioning, and cross-model compatibility.

Dexploarer: Focused on enhancing cross-platform stability and environment configuration, successfully merging PR #8074 in elizaos/eliza to implement out-of-box whitelabeling. They also maintained project integrity by opening three additional PRs (#8103, #8102, #8068) to address installer naming and submodule dependency management, centering their efforts entirely on critical bugfixes and infrastructure maintenance.

lalalune: Focused on stabilizing the elizaos/eliza codebase by opening four pull requests addressing UI testing, workspace connector error handling, CI loader paths, and agentic view surfaces (#8088, #8087, #8084, #8080). Their efforts were further supported by providing 7 PR comments to facilitate progress across these key infrastructure and interface improvements.

odilitime: Focused on critical security hardening, successfully merging PR #8076 in elizaos/eliza to secure destructive actions, MCP spawning, and web functionality. This extensive effort involved modifying 228 files with over 11,000 total lines of code, demonstrating a primary focus on high-impact bugfix and security work.

SYMBaiEX: Focused on infrastructure stability by opening PR #8104 in elizaos/eliza to address native agent sandbox routing. This effort involved extensive codebase modifications across 2,385 files, with a primary emphasis on bugfixes and testing.

cyphercodes: Focused on enhancing project reliability by implementing smoke testing for global package installations in elizaos/eliza (#8075). This contribution involved 108 lines of code changes, underscoring a dedicated effort toward improving testing infrastructure.
